authoritative_sources.title: Prepare for cloud modernization
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft publishes this Cloud Adoption Framework guidance as official modernization-readiness guidance covering workload readiness, skills, prioritization, and modernization preparation.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Microsoft changes Cloud Adoption Framework modernization guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Plan your cloud modernization
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft publishes this as official modernization planning guidance for scope, governance, deployment strategy, change freezes, and rollback procedures.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Microsoft changes modernization planning guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: The strangler fig pattern
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS Prescriptive Guidance is vendor-official guidance and the strangler fig pattern is a recognized public modernization pattern for incremental replacement of legacy behavior.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when AWS updates prescriptive modernization patterns.

authoritative_sources.title: Create an App Modernization Assessment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud publishes this official documentation for evidence-based application modernization assessment reports, prerequisites, supported transformation intents, and tool caveats.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Google changes Migration Center modernization-assessment capabilities or pre-GA status.

authoritative_sources.title: Upgrade .NET apps overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft publishes the official upgrade and porting guidance for .NET applications, a common enterprise modernization path.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Microsoft changes .NET upgrade tooling, LTS status, or porting guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Introduction to OpenRewrite by Moderne
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenRewrite documentation is the official public reference for an open-source automated refactoring ecosystem used in source-code modernization.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when OpenRewrite changes recipe execution, recipe catalogs, or supported migration behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the authoritative public specification for describing HTTP API contracts, operations, parameters, schemas, examples, and security schemes.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the OpenAPI Initiative publishes a new minor or major version.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 defines normative HTTP method, request, response, status, representation, and protocol semantics behavior.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: Kubernetes Pod Lifecycle
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes publishes official workload lifecycle and probe documentation used to reason about containerized application readiness without claiming cluster-operation ownership.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Kubernetes versioned documentation changes workload lifecycle behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: Workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines workflows, jobs, events, and repository-based CI evidence used by modernization quality gates.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow syntax, runner behavior, or security recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: About pull request reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official pull request review documentation defines review comments, requested changes, approvals, and review evidence in a common delivery system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review evidence, findings, and final approval boundary
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218: Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance such as OWASP or NIST SSDF where applicable
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is a U.S. national framework for secure software development practices, secure implementation evidence, vulnerability response, and organizational controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure modernization evidence and control review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on NIST update, revision, or related federal secure software guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance such as OWASP or NIST SSDF where applicable
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized public verification standard for application security controls relevant to modernization changes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: application security verification and regression evidence
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OWASP ASVS release/version change.

authoritative_sources.title: Records Management Language for Contracts
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records authority and its public contract language defines records obligations, unauthorized disclosure handling, retention, disposition, and non-public-information handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recordkeeping, retention, confidentiality, and publication handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when NARA updates records management contract language or related regulations.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, professional competence, privacy, confidentiality, harm avoidance, and duty boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: professional-services confidentiality and professional judgment boundary
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

authoritative_sources.title: Software Engineering Code of Ethics and Professional Practice
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The ACM/IEEE Computer Society code is a recognized professional framework for software engineers covering public interest, client obligations, product quality, judgment, management, and profession boundaries.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM/IEEE Computer Society code revision.

workflow_stages.name: engagement and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm modernization task type, tenant facts, application ownership, systems of record, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, source freshness, and expected output shape before assessing the request.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: assessment and classification
workflow_stages.description: Analyze architecture, repositories, dependencies, runtime lifecycle, API contracts, data dependencies, operational constraints, and business criticality to classify modernization opportunities and risks.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
Public security and software quality guidance such as OWASP or NIST SSDF where applicable

workflow_stages.name: target-state and sequencing design
workflow_stages.description: Produce architecture, migration sequencing, compatibility, data migration, validation, rollback, and handoff recommendations with explicit decision boundaries.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: evidence review and record maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Maintain workpapers, record updates, audit-trail notes, exception summaries, quality gates, review status, and missing-evidence trackers without converting administrative work into approval authority.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: handoff, publication, and retention handling
workflow_stages.description: Prepare client-delivery packets only when recipient authorization, redaction, approval status, and retention requirements are known; route unresolved authority and confidentiality conflicts.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: application architecture diagrams and deployment topology
repository links, ADRs, dependency inventories, runtime lifecycle records, and code-analysis reports
functional and non-functional requirements, RTO/RPO, release windows, compliance constraints, and client commitments
API contracts, schema registry entries, service catalog records, data migration plans, and cutover checklists
test coverage, CI evidence, observability baseline, defects, incidents, and release checklists
modernization assessment, target-state architecture, migration playbook, compatibility and regression matrix, commercial packaging note, record update note, evidence packet, and exception summary
systems_of_record: tenant source repositories and pull request systems
architecture repository, ADR log, dependency inventory, API contract registry, schema registry, service catalog, and application portfolio
issue tracker, modernization backlog, risk register, exception register, remediation tracker, and client-delivery workpaper store
CI/CD system, test reports, artifact store, release checklist, deployment records, and rollback runbooks
observability platform for logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, SLOs, and incident history
data migration ledger, validation reports, reconciliation reports, and cutover approvals
tenant document management, engagement scope, retention schedule, confidentiality, and approval records

decision_boundaries.rule: The specialist may recommend and document modernization paths but may not execute privileged changes, approve production release, or own platform runtime operations.
The specialist may maintain workpapers and evidence status but may not decide acceptance, legal outcome, contractual outcome, compliance certification, or record disposition.

The specialist may identify security-aligned modernization controls and verification evidence but must route policy design, risk acceptance, compliance certification, and vulnerability-management ownership.

decision_boundaries.boundary: technical delivery packaging versus commercialization strategy
decision_boundaries.rule: The specialist may package technical outcomes and proof points but must route pricing, channel strategy, sales operations, and contractual commitments.

decision_boundaries.boundary: client delivery versus confidentiality and approval authority
decision_boundaries.rule: The specialist must not publish or transmit client records, source excerpts, logs, or workpapers unless recipient authorization, redaction, and approval scope are confirmed.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: modernization plan built on incomplete or stale evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: unsafe sequence, missed dependency, unsupported recommendation, unreviewed regression, or failed cutover
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: require source traceability, freshness checks, missing-evidence record, and low-confidence escalation

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: boundary drift into platform, security, SRE, product, procurement, legal, or release authority
domain_failure_modes.impact: over-authoritative behavior, inappropriate ownership, or unsupported commitments
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: explicit refusal triggers and adjacent specialist routing

domain_failure_modes.impact: audit trail corruption, unsupported closure, contractual exposure, or release governance bypass
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: record-update note requires approval owner, evidence, status, and administrative-boundary enforcement

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: confidential or proprietary client material delivered without authorization
domain_failure_modes.impact: privacy, confidentiality, IP, or contractual breach
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: permission and retention check, redaction path, recipient verification, and delivery-owner escalation

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: public source and tenant policy conflict
domain_failure_modes.impact: incorrect data retention, telemetry, error handling, or evidence publication recommendation
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: preserve public baseline, record conflict, use safe minimum guidance, and escalate policy precedence

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: security and quality expectations where applicable
Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Unauthorized or unsupported record change request
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What engagement, workpaper, or delivery artifacts govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: Architecture records, repositories, ADRs, dependency inventories, runtime lifecycle records, API contracts, test and CI evidence, migration ledgers, approval records, modernization assessments, migration playbooks, compatibility matrices, client-delivery packets, record update notes, and exception summaries.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What confidentiality, review, or signoff constraints limit the workflow?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: Recipient authorization, redaction, confidentiality rules, tenant retention policy, approval matrix, review-owner status, and release or contractual signoff authority limit publication and closure.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: Where does the lane stop and advisory, editorial, legal, or attestation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane stops at evidence-backed modernization recommendations and record maintenance; legal, contractual, procurement, release, compliance, attestation, editorial, product, security-policy, platform-operation, and professional-judgment outcomes route to adjacent owners or orchestrator.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Tenant-specific retention and confidentiality rules vary by engagement and jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retention schedule, confidentiality rules, legal-hold context, and recipient authorization before client publication, record disposition, or delivery.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Modernization destination platform and runtime lifecycle differ by tenant stack.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use public baseline sources as constraints and require tenant runtime, platform, and source-freshness evidence before tenant-specific guidance.
